hero.title: Four regulations. One evidence system.
hero.subtitle: OXOT unifies the Cyber Resilience Act, the AI Act, the Machinery Regulation and IEC 62443 into a single, living source of conformity evidence — so your teams prove compliance instead of chasing it.

logoWall.title: Built for the regulations reshaping industrial products

featureGrid.title: Everything a conformity dossier needs, in one place
featureGrid.subtitle: Stop maintaining four parallel spreadsheets. OXOT models the shared reality underneath every regulation and keeps it current as your product evolves.

features.title: Unified control library
features.description: One requirement model spanning CRA, AI Act, Machinery and IEC 62443 — mapped, deduplicated and versioned.

features.title: Living evidence
features.description: Link tickets, tests, SBOMs and design docs to controls. Evidence updates as your product does.

features.title: Gap detection
features.description: See exactly which obligations are covered, partial or open — per product, per regulation, in real time.

features.title: Audit-ready export
features.description: Generate a structured technical file and declaration of conformity your notified body can actually follow.

features.title: Risk & threat modeling
features.description: Structured risk assessments aligned to IEC 62443 and the AI Act, reusable across product lines.

features.title: Expert in the loop
features.description: OXOT consultants review your dossier and coach your team — the platform never leaves you alone with the hard calls.

problem.title: The regulations overlap. Your tooling doesn't.
problem.body: CRA wants secure-by-design evidence. IEC 62443 wants the same, in its own language. The Machinery Regulation adds safety. The AI Act adds risk management. Most teams answer each one separately — and pay for the same work four times.
bullets: Evidence that goes stale the moment code ships
No single view of what is actually covered

shift.title: Prove conformity as a by-product of building
shift.body: OXOT maps every obligation to a shared control once, then wires those controls to the evidence your teams already produce. Compliance stops being a project and becomes a property of how you work.
bullets: Engineers contribute evidence without leaving their tools
Leadership sees conformity status at a glance

comparison.title: One system versus four silos
comparison.subtitle: What changes when overlapping regulations share a single evidence backbone.

steps.title: From scattered documents to a living dossier
Scope
steps.description: We map your products to the regulations that apply and the controls they share.

steps.title: Connect
steps.description: Evidence sources — tests, SBOMs, risk assessments — are wired into the control model.

steps.title: Close gaps
steps.description: OXOT surfaces what's open; our experts help you close it efficiently.

steps.title: Sustain
steps.description: The dossier stays current automatically, ready for audit at any moment.

quote.quote: We used to treat every regulation as a separate fire drill. With OXOT the evidence writes itself once and answers all of them.
quote.author: Head of Product Security

items.question: Which regulations does OXOT cover?
items.answer: The Cyber Resilience Act, the EU AI Act, the Machinery Regulation (2023/1230) and IEC 62443, with adjacent frameworks like NIS2 mapped in.

items.question: Is this software or consulting?
items.answer: Both. The platform maintains your evidence; OXOT experts review dossiers and guide your team through the hard decisions.

items.question: Do our engineers have to learn a new tool?
items.answer: Minimally. Evidence is pulled from the systems they already use, so contribution happens in the flow of their work.

items.question: How quickly can we be audit-ready?
items.answer: Most teams reach an audit-ready dossier in weeks rather than quarters, because overlapping work is only done once.

cta.title: See your conformity status in one view
cta.subtitle: Book a walkthrough and we'll map your products to the regulations that matter.
